data.title: ECB's Villeroy: Private Credit Poses Both Risk and Opportunity for EU
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member François Villeroy de Galhau has highlighted the dual nature of private credit within the European Union. According to Jin10, Villeroy emphasized that while private credit presents certain risks, it also offers significant opportunities for the EU's financial landscape. He noted that the management of these risks is crucial to harnessing the potential benefits. Villeroy's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the role of private credit in the broader economic framework of the EU. The ECB continues to monitor the situation closely, aiming to strike a balance between fostering growth and maintaining financial stability.

data.title: ECB's Villeroy: Private Equity Can Boost EU Economy and Innovation
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member François Villeroy de Galhau has emphasized the potential of private equity to enhance the European Union's economic growth and innovation. According to Jin10, Villeroy highlighted the role of private equity in fostering economic development and driving innovation across the EU. He noted that private equity investments could provide essential support to emerging businesses and innovative projects, thereby contributing to the overall economic landscape. Villeroy's remarks underscore the importance of private equity as a catalyst for economic progress within the European Union.

data.title: Iran says a few tweets from Trump won't end the war.
data.description: On March 12, local time, Iranian Supreme National Security Council Secretary Larijani, commenting on US President Trump's statement that "this war must be won quickly," pointed out that starting a war is easy, but ending one cannot be achieved with a few tweets. Larijani stated that once a war begins, ending it is not easy. He also emphasized that Iran will never let its adversaries off easily. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Slovakia's Fertilizer Industry Faces Challenges Amid Rising Gas Prices
data.description: Slovakia's leading fertilizer manufacturer has announced a reduction in ammonia production due to a significant increase in natural gas prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the Middle East conflict on European industries. The surge in gas prices is affecting various sectors, with the fertilizer industry being one of the hardest hit. This development underscores the broader economic repercussions of geopolitical tensions, as energy costs continue to rise across the continent.

data.title: Amazon Bonds Deemed Safe Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description: Amazon is set to sell $54 billion in bonds this week, as tensions in Iran escalate. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that banks are promoting Amazon as a reliable investment in an increasingly unpredictable global landscape. The bond sale underscores Amazon's perceived stability and resilience, even as geopolitical tensions rise. Investors are drawn to the company's strong market position and consistent performance, making it an attractive option during uncertain times.

data.title: Asian Defense Officials Worry Over Potential Shift in U.S. Military Focus
data.description: Current and former defense officials in Asia are increasingly worried about the potential reallocation of American military resources if the ongoing conflict continues. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting these concerns as the war persists, potentially leading to a shift in U.S. military focus away from Asia. This apprehension stems from the possibility that prolonged engagement in the conflict could necessitate a redistribution of American firepower, impacting the strategic balance in the region. The officials emphasize the importance of maintaining a robust U.S. military presence in Asia to ensure regional stability and security. As the situation unfolds, the implications for defense strategies in Asia remain a significant point of discussion among military leaders.

data.title: Senator Kaine Anticipates Early Report on Iran School Attack Soon
data.description: U.S. Senator Tim Kaine has indicated that an initial report concerning the attack on a girls' school in Iran is expected to be released shortly. According to Jin10, while the report is anticipated soon, it will not be available today. The incident has drawn international attention, prompting calls for a thorough investigation into the circumstances surrounding the attack.

data.title: California's Child Online Privacy Law Faces Partial Setback in Appeals Court
data.description: A U.S. appeals court has rejected an injunction aimed at preventing California from enforcing its child online privacy law. According to Jin10, the court ruled that while California can proceed with implementing the law, it does not have the authority to enforce certain provisions. This decision marks a significant development in the ongoing legal battle over online privacy regulations, highlighting the complexities of state versus federal jurisdiction in digital privacy matters. The ruling may influence future legislative efforts and enforcement strategies concerning online privacy protections for minors.

data.title: The US Senate passed a bill with provisions that would ban the issuance of CBDCs, with the ban potentially lasting until 2030.
data.description: The US Senate passed a bipartisan housing bill by a vote of 89 to 10. The bill includes a provision prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), a ban that will remain in effect until at least the end of 2030. It requires the Federal Reserve not to issue or create CBDCs or similar digital assets directly or through intermediaries such as financial institutions. However, the bill's prospects in the House of Representatives remain uncertain. Some lawmakers have objected to provisions limiting the number of homes that large institutional investors can own, which could lead to a new version of the bill being considered in the House, thus affecting its progress. Industry insiders say the provisions reinforce the stance of private sector-led digital asset innovation and the protection of financial privacy. (Washingtonexaminer)

data.title: Cyber Group Handala Claims Responsibility for Attacks on Israeli Firms
data.description: A cyber group known as Handala has announced its involvement in recent attacks targeting Israeli companies. Bloomberg posted on X that Handala has taken credit for the disruption of operations at the Israeli payments company Verifone, following its earlier claim of responsibility for the Stryker attack. The group's actions have raised concerns about cybersecurity vulnerabilities within the affected firms. The incidents highlight the ongoing challenges faced by companies in safeguarding their digital infrastructure against cyber threats.

data.title: U.S. Senate Advances Bill to Restrict Federal Reserve's CBDC Issuance
data.description: The U.S. Senate has moved forward with the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, passing it with an 89–10 vote. According to NS3.AI, a significant provision within the bill aims to prevent the Federal Reserve from issuing a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) until at least the end of 2030. This restriction would apply to both direct and intermediary issuance of a CBDC. However, the bill's future remains uncertain as House lawmakers may propose amendments to the broader housing legislation, potentially delaying or obstructing the CBDC ban.

data.title: TRON Surpasses Ethereum in USDT Circulation Following Recent Minting
data.description: Tether has minted an additional 1 billion USDT on the TRON network, increasing the circulating supply of USDT on TRON to over $85.3 billion. This development positions TRON ahead of Ethereum in terms of USDT circulation. According to NS3.AI, the weekly stablecoin transfer volume on TRON has grown significantly, rising from below $80 billion to $160 billion over the past three years. In a related legal update, a judge dismissed the SEC lawsuit against the Tron Foundation and Justin Sun with prejudice in March.

data.title: Heating Oil Prices in the U.S. Surpass $5 per Gallon
data.description: Heating oil prices in the United States have exceeded $5 per gallon, marking a significant increase. According to Jin10, this price surge is attributed to various factors impacting the energy market. The rise in heating oil prices is likely to affect consumers as the winter season approaches, potentially leading to higher heating costs for households. The situation is being closely monitored by market analysts and policymakers, who are assessing the potential implications for the broader economy.

data.title: Cyberattack Disrupts Stryker's Operations
data.description: A recent cyberattack has severely impacted Stryker, a medical technology company, causing its ordering and shipping systems to remain offline. Bloomberg posted on X that the company is grappling with the aftermath of a significant hack, which has been attributed to a group associated with Iran. The disruption has posed challenges for Stryker as it works to restore its systems and resume normal operations. The company has not provided a timeline for when the systems will be fully operational again. This incident highlights the growing threat of cyberattacks on critical infrastructure and the need for robust cybersecurity measures.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: The average mortgage rate in the United States has increased to 6.11%, driven by ongoing inflation concerns linked to the conflict with Iran. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on financial markets. This rise in mortgage rates reflects broader economic uncertainties as investors react to the potential implications of the conflict on global oil supplies and inflationary pressures. The situation remains fluid, with market participants closely monitoring developments in the region and their potential effects on the U.S. economy.

data.title: Zealand to Establish Research Facility in Boston to Enhance U.S. Presence
data.description: Zealand Pharma is set to open a new research facility in Boston as part of its strategy to strengthen its footprint in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's focus on expanding its operations in the U.S. market. The facility will support Zealand's efforts in developing obesity treatments, aligning with its broader mission to address global health challenges. This move is expected to bolster Zealand's research capabilities and foster collaborations within the U.S. scientific community. The expansion reflects the company's commitment to innovation and growth in the pharmaceutical sector.

data.title: U.S. Reports Damage to Over 60 Iranian Vessels
data.description: The United States has reported that more than 60 Iranian vessels, including over 30 mine-laying ships, have been damaged or destroyed. According to Jin10, this development highlights ongoing tensions in the region. The situation underscores the complex geopolitical dynamics at play, with potential implications for regional stability and international relations. Further details on the incidents or the broader context were not provided in the report.

data.title: Ethena plans to adjust the sUSDe unstaking cooldown period to a dynamic mechanism, reducing it to as short as 1 day.
data.description: Ethena announced on its X platform that it plans to adjust the sUSDe unstaking cooldown period from a fixed 7 days to a dynamic mechanism, linked to the proportion of liquid stablecoins in the USDe reserves. Under the new mechanism, the current sUSDe unstaking waiting period will be shortened from 7 days to 1 day, and the future cooldown period will fluctuate between 1 and 7 days, depending on the proportion of more liquid assets in the USDe reserves.

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 1,965 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 19,243 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 1,965 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 19,243 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 37,479 SOL.

data.title: AI's Influence on Software Industry Credit Ratings Expected to Be Limited, Says S&P
data.description: The software industry is unlikely to experience widespread credit rating downgrades due to the impact of artificial intelligence, according to S&P. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the credit rating agency does not foresee AI advancements causing significant disruptions in the sector. S&P's analysis suggests that while AI will continue to influence the industry, it will not lead to a substantial decline in credit ratings across the board. The agency's outlook remains stable, indicating confidence in the software sector's ability to adapt to technological changes without major financial setbacks.
